title: peperomia santa-elisae
intro: Peperomia santa-elisae, also known as the 'Baby Rubber Plant', is a low-maintenance, tropical houseplant species belonging to the Piperaceae family. Traditionally, the roots of Peperomia santa-elisae have been used in herbal medicine, valued for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, the roots of Peperomia santa-elisae are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to possess medicinal properties when consumed under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
